comments.author: @roberthutchison8197
comments.text: The western part gets all the glory but the rest is mostly open plains with only two mountain ranges, the Sweet Grass Hills and the Bear Paws, both volcanic in origin,.  There are hardly any freeways with most of the roads two lanes and you can drive for hours and hardly meet much traffic.  And the town are far apart and when you drive, have have to keep your eyes on the gas gage because not every town has a gas station.  And the places to stay are also almost as scarce as the gas stations!  But here is where Montana really gets its name, BIG SKY COUNTRY.  Nothing to block the view and the clear sky above...

comments.author: @ichneu
comments.text:
Beautiful images, just like in all your videos. Thanks. Always amazed by the USA, including Montana. Montana is just over 9 times the size of my country (the Netherlands) and has 1/18 of our population.
The Dutch economy is about 20 times larger than Montana's, which isn't even bad when you consider that most of Montana is uninhabitable for humans.
Just imagine: the surface area of ​​our largest nature reserve is 1,000 km² (386 square miles). Maybe I should move to Montana someday?

comments.author: @tigerpisces5506
comments.text: Correction Yellow Stone river is not the longest undamned river. The Salmon River in Idaho, famously nicknamed the "River of No Return," is the longest undammed river in the contiguous United States, flowing for 425 miles entirely within the state. It is a world-renowned destination for whitewater rafting, blue-ribbon fly fishing, and exploring one of the deepest river gorges in North America.
